title: clinacanthus burmanni
intro: Clinacanthus burmanni, also known as Sabdariffa or Bitter Balsam, is a perennial plant native to Thailand and other parts of Southeast Asia, characterized by its vibrant red flowers and distinctive bitter taste. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Clinacanthus burmanni include reducing fever and inflammation, treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, and even aiding in weight loss. The plant is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, and its leaves can also be dried and used in herbal remedies, while the flowers are used as a garnish or in traditional medicine.
